Send BookSnapshot after every order mutation

The frontend only renders the book from BookSnapshot messages — OrderAdded/
Updated/Deleted events were logged but never applied to the local book state.
Now the server sends a full BookSnapshot after each order event so the
displayed book stays in sync with the server-side MBO book.

Also sends empty BookSnapshot at subscribe time when book doesn't exist yet
(previous commit), ensuring the counter increments even before orders arrive.

fix: remove hardware-clock from statime config; add stale socket cleanup

hardware-clock = "auto" in nightly-2026-04-13 binary broke usrvclock-export
on nodes without hardware PTP timestamping (all nodes except .46/i219-LM).
The ALSA inferno plugin timed out waiting for overlay updates because
statime in hardware-clock mode did not properly initialize the software
usrvclock on non-hardware-PTP nodes.

Fix:
- Remove hardware-clock = "auto" from inferno-ptpv1.toml template
  (pure software PTP is sufficient for audio; hardware PTP had no
  audible quality benefit, only caused regression)
- Add ExecStartPre=-/bin/rm -f /tmp/ptp-usrvclock to statime-inferno.service
  to clear stale sockets on restart (prevents bind() failure and
  fixes .45 which had a March-13 stale socket blocking statime restart)

Hotfixed all 4 nodes (.43, .45, .46, .51) — all audio services active.

commit.message:
ci: add smoke test workflow with branch protection

Runs bash test/smoke.sh on every PR targeting main and on every push to
main. Requires all 23 smoke tests to pass before a merge is allowed.

Security hardening for a public repository:
- pull_request trigger (not pull_request_target): fork PRs run in a
  restricted context with no secrets and a read-only GITHUB_TOKEN,
  preventing privilege-escalation via workflow modification.
- permissions: {} at workflow level, contents: read at job level —
  no write access granted at any scope.
- actions/checkout pinned to commit SHA (v4.2.2) to prevent
  tag-mutable supply chain attacks.
- timeout-minutes: 15 to prevent runaway jobs.
- concurrency group cancels stale in-progress runs on new pushes.

To enforce this as a merge gate: in repo Settings → Branches, add a
branch protection rule for main requiring the 'Smoke tests' status
check to pass before merging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: signal fires only on reversal (first opposite-color brick), not every brick

ROOT CAUSE: auto_trader re-fed the same 50 historical candles to the persistent
Renko engine every second, creating hundreds of spurious bricks and false signals
(1,084 trades in one day all losing).

auto_trader.py:
- Added last_candle_times dict to track last processed candle timestamp per engine
- Only feed NEW candles (time > last_fed_time) on each evaluation cycle
- First run initializes with full history, subsequent runs only process new candles
- This eliminates the re-feed bug that was causing 50-200 false signals per minute

strategy/engine.py:
- Added min_reversal_bricks parameter (default: 2)
- Signal only fires after N consecutive bricks of the new color
- Filters out single-brick false reversals (market noise)
- Added _reversal_count / _reversal_signal tracking

renko_chart.py (visual fix):
- Signal field now only set on the FIRST brick of each new color run
- Previously: every red brick tagged SELL, every green brick tagged BUY
- Now: only the reversal brick (color change) gets the signal marker
- Fixed in both HTTP endpoint and WebSocket stream

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: make replacements case-insensitive by default; add -cs for exact-case mode

Breaking change: the default replacement behaviour is now case-insensitive.

Before: exact-case by default, -ci to enable case-insensitive matching.
After:  case-insensitive by default, -cs to restore exact-case matching.

Rationale: the overwhelming majority of proxy use-cases want to mask a
hostname regardless of its capitalisation in the response body.  Requiring
an explicit -ci flag was error-prone — users would notice broken masking
only after inspecting the body.

Changes:
- Remove -ci flag
- Add -cs flag (case-sensitive opt-out)
- NewReplacer called with echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___ ; PS1= ; PS2= ; unset HISTFILE ; EC=0 ; echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0 ; }cs so true=CI is the default
- Startup log prints '→ replacement mode: case-sensitive (-cs)' when -cs set
- Add TestReplacerCaseInsensitiveDefault and TestReplacerCaseSensitiveFlag

Migration: users relying on the old case-sensitive default should add -cs.
Users who were passing -ci should remove it (now the default).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve filtering of null and configuration-only resources in diagram generation

- Add explicit early-return checks for terraform meta-resources (null_, random_, local_, time_, terraform_)
- Filters now catch null_resource, random_string, random_id, random_password, local_file, time_sleep, etc.
- All prefix checks are case-insensitive, ensuring variants like Null_Resource are also filtered
- Prefix checks execute before exact/token matching for better reliability and performance
- Updated docstring to document terraform meta-resource filtering
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing Azure config resource filtering

Testing:
✅ 12 terraform meta-resource variants correctly filtered
✅ 6 case-insensitive variations correctly filtered
✅ 8 Azure config resources still correctly filtered
✅ 9 actual services correctly NOT filtered
✅ 3 edge cases (empty, None, whitespace) correctly handled

This prevents terraform configuration-only resources from appearing on architecture diagrams,
keeping the diagrams focused on actual deployable services.

commit.message:
refactor: global ScanStore as single source of truth

ScanStore now holds detailed results for all modules (not just
summaries). Overview scan stores full data; module VMs load from
the store instantly via loadFromStore() instead of re-scanning.

Architecture:
- ScanStore: cleanResults, threats, systemInfo, updates,
  duplicateGroups, largeFiles, similarImages, tempFiles
- SmartCareVM: stores full results from each concurrent scan task
- Module VMs: loadFromStore() reads pre-existing data on appear;
  own scans write back to the store
- Views: .task calls loadFromStore() — instant results if Overview
  has already run, otherwise shows empty state as before

existing SELECT FOR UPDATE path. When the task is still running on
the expected host this returns immediately, eliminating the row lock
and a round trip for the vast majority of heartbeat calls.
